title: Kaiseki-Style Pork Cutlet with Crispy Breading
description: A Japanese-inspired dish featuring a tender pork cutlet coated in a crispy, flavorful breading. Perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: 1. In a shallow dish, mix together flour, salt, and pepper.
2. In a separate dish, beat the egg.
3. In a third dish, place the breadcrumbs.
4. Dip each pork cutlet in the flour mixture, coating both sides.
5. Dip the floured pork cutlet in the beaten egg, making sure to coat completely.
6. Finally, coat the pork cutlet in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
7. Heat about 1/4 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
8. Fry the breaded pork cutlets for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
9. Remove the pork cutlets from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
10. Serve hot with a drizzle of sesame oil and your choice of steamed vegetables or rice.
